semantic_description: The target function performs in-place insertion sort over an integer array a of length n. It preserves the array length and mutates the caller-owned IntArray so that the final list lr is sorted according to insertion_sort_spec and is a permutation of the original input list l. The implementation has an outer for loop from i = 1 while i < n and an inner while loop that shifts larger prefix elements right until the insertion point for key is found. The loop may be skipped for n <= 1 or when the current key is already in order; memory is modified only through writes to a[j + 1].
